steps.title: Identify the required clause structure
steps.content: The opening “Compared to that of the direct imaging method,” sets up a comparison that requires a direct statement about the radial velocity method, followed by explanation of why it has this feature.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for clause boundaries
steps.content: B properly separates the main comparison (“the radial velocity method's zone of discovery is vast”) from a supporting clause by using a comma and the subordinating conjunction as. This follows the standard convention for marking clear boundaries between an independent clause and a dependent explanatory clause. The other choices either lack this clear boundary marking by combining ideas into one clause (D), form awkward noun phrases (A), or contain grammatical errors with preposition usage (C). That matches choice B — B is correct.
